Experience Audit · March 2026
Burcak Okcu · Product Design · Zendesk

Can an admin confidently adopt, operate, and prove the value of AI Agents — without stitching the experience together themselves?

The answer today: Not easily.

Scroll to explore
The Admin

Before we look at the system — who is actually navigating it?

0
% of AI admins spend most of their time manually testing AI features — their #1 activity, with no dedicated tooling
0
% cite optimizing AI output quality as their top challenge — they're improving what they can't measure
0
% find setting up AI tools difficult or very difficult — the lowest ease score of any admin task
85%
also configure Zendesk settings
82%
also build dashboards & reports
72%
also manage queues & tickets
71%
also manage knowledge base content

Source: Understanding Admins — survey 2025, Alicja Antkowiak (n≈850). This reframes the cost of fragmentation: every extra step, every context switch, is a tax on someone who's already stretched thin.

What we found

Four structural patterns

These aren't individual bugs. They persist and compound — even as individual features improve.

🧩
Jobs split across tools
Admins visit 3–5 separate surfaces to answer a single question about their AI Agent. Time lost, trust eroded, context constantly broken.
👁️
System state is invisible
No unified view of what the AI Agent is doing right now or why it made a specific decision. Troubleshooting becomes guesswork.
📈
Optimization over outcomes
Setup flows measure completion percentage. Not whether the AI Agent is actually performing well after launch.
❓
Unclear ownership
When something goes wrong, no clear surface owns the resolution path. A failed escalation could be routing rules, a knowledge gap, or model behavior — each in a different tool.

Burcak Okcu · Product Design · Zendesk · March 2026

Where focus creates impact

Product & experience overlaps

The same concepts are configured in multiple places — admins navigate duplicated, disconnected surfaces to accomplish a single goal.

Concept In AI Agents Also in Impact
Instructions Instructions Admin Center: Communication Guidelines Admins configure the same behaviour in two separate places, risking conflicting rules that are invisible to each other and hard to debug.
Procedures Procedures Knowledge: Procedures The same label describes different features in two products, causing admins to build the wrong type of flow and lose time diagnosing why it doesn't work.
Operation Hours Agent Settings Admin Center: Objects & Rules (Schedules) Admins miss this setting at launch because it lives outside the agent setup flow, leading to AI agents responding outside business hours — a frequent post-launch incident.
Actions Actions Admin Center: Apps & Integrations Action setup is split across products, forcing admins to context-switch mid-task and increasing the chance of misconfiguration that breaks integrations silently.
Entities Entities Admin Center: Intelligent Triage Two distinct entity systems with overlapping purpose but no connection — admins set up extraction in both places without knowing the relationship, leading to duplicate or conflicting data extraction.
Intent / Use Cases Use Cases Admin Center: Intelligent Triage (Intents) The same concept — classifying what the customer wants — lives under two different names in two products. Admins building AI-powered routing configure intent in both without a unified view of coverage or conflicts.
Trial Experience Trial onboarding & test flows Admin Center: Trial account setup Trial users encounter disconnected setup journeys across AI Agents and Admin Center, leading to early abandonment before they see first value — directly impacting trial-to-paid conversion.
Routing & Escalation Escalation configuration Admin Center: Routing (skills, groups, triggers) Routing logic that determines when and where a conversation escalates is split across two products with no unified view — gaps between them cause conversations to get stranded.
Critical business gaps

Overlaps compound into these three systemic gaps — each validated by design leads closest to the problem.

Critical
Fragmented monitoring
Answering "is my AI Agent working?" requires visiting 3–4 separate surfaces. No unified health view exists. The fragmentation is confirmed even within a single app — AutoQA and BotQA are split across separate dashboards inside QA.
⚠ Data needed — reach out to Andy for avg surfaces visited per monitoring session
" The experience of having comprehensive reporting on AI Agents is fragmented even inside the QA app — AutoQA and BotQA are split across separate dashboards.
— Pirje Tumm, Design Manager, QA
High
Post-onboarding cliff
After guided unified onboarding, admins land in AI Agents with no clear path for what to configure next. The gap between the guided experience and the actual product complexity is stark — and each additional surface makes the drop steeper.
Affects every customer. Workarounds exist but require tribal knowledge.
" When considering AI setup at large, you have many places to analyse conversations: QA, AI Agents, Agent Workspace for Copilot — and you'd need to visit all of them.
— Kevin Gauthier, Design Director
High
Naming & taxonomy chaos
"Use cases", "procedures", "flows", "system replies", "default replies" — inconsistent naming across and within products erodes admin confidence, increases cognitive load, and makes the product feel unfinished.
Flagged independently by Caroline Murphy and Piotr Bajbak. No consistency with Auto-assist either.
" Use cases, dialogue builder, flows, procedures, system replies — the different naming conventions are so confusing. We really need to prioritise the IA work to align the two experiences.
— Caroline Murphy, Lead Designer
High
Widget branding not in setup flow
Enterprise
Enterprise clients must configure widget branding and authentication before launch — but this sits entirely outside the AI Agent onboarding flow. Admins discover this gap post-launch and must backtrack. Channel selection happens during AI setup; widget configuration does not.
Raised by Monika (Messaging Designer). No cross-linking between widget settings and AI Agent setup.
" Enterprise launches without proper branding, must backtrack to add widget styling and authentication — every time.
— Monika, Messaging Designer

What's already moving

In flight
Q2 — Planned
ZRA + HyperArc
AI Agents data coming to ZRA in Q2, alongside HyperArc reports — addressing multi-surface monitoring fragmentation.
In design
Knowledge Copilot
Unified knowledge management surface in progress — addressing the split between AI Agents and Knowledge for procedure and content management.
In progress
Pricing × Reporting
New resolution pricing tiers being aligned with reporting dashboards. Cross-team collaboration underway.
Ongoing
Homepage Dashboard Unification
Admin Center rebuilding the dashboard to improve essential setup experience and simplify widget and messaging configuration — directly addressing fragmented setup surfaces.
In discussion
AI Agent Standalone Strategy
Revising messaging personas and positioning for AI Agents sold standalone (not bundled). Changes how channels and messaging are presented in the admin journey.

Now we want to hear from you

Your turn

You have seen the journey, the gaps, and what is already in motion. This map is more useful with your perspective in it — what you are seeing from your area, what is missing, and where you think the biggest opportunities are.

🗺
Does this match what you are seeing?
Flag anything that feels off, incomplete, or that you would frame differently based on your area of the product.
🔍
What gaps are you sitting on?
If your team has identified pain points or experience debt not captured here, add it. This map should reflect what we collectively know.
📍
Where does your 2026 roadmap connect?
Point to the steps or gaps where your planned work closes something — or where it leaves something open. No extra work required, just context.
Leave a comment on the Figma board ↗

Or reach out to Burcak directly — questions, pushback, and additions all welcome.

The goal: move from building a lot of good things
to building one coherent AI Agent experience.
